title: ADK Recipe Manifest
description: Schema for manifest.yaml files in core/ and contrib/ directories

type.description: The recipe type. 'standalone': a complete, runnable recipe with its own entry point that can be run or deployed independently (e.g. a fully functioning IT Helpdesk Agent, an OAuth 2.0 multi-agent routing guide). 'module': a packaged, importable sub-agent designed to be orchestrated by a larger workflow, but lacking a standalone entry point to run on its own (e.g. a specialized BigQuery query-generator sub-agent).

deployable.description: Whether the recipe can be deployed with one click. false (default): not possible to deploy with one click. true: possible to deploy with one click.

large.description: Whether the recipe is considered large in size. false (default): under 1MB, 50 files max. true: under 10MB, 200 files max.

language.description: Primary programming language of the recipe.

description.description: A clear description of the recipe and the value it provides.

architecture.description: Optional fields describing the agent architecture of the recipe. All sub-fields are optional.

agent.description: Whether the recipe uses a single agent or multiple agents.

stateful.description: Whether the agent maintains persistent state or memory across sessions, or performs transactional writes to external systems.

datasources.description: The data sources used by the recipe. 'hardcoded': data is hardcoded within the recipe itself. 'local': data comes from files bundled with the recipe. 'external': data comes from a live outside system at runtime (e.g. a database, an API).

rag.description: Whether the recipe implements or uses a RAG pipeline.
